source: Ultimate Wynncraft Lootrun Guide — Lootrun Camps tab
why: Camp is a REQUIRED run-setup input: Chronokinesis only counts environmental and Spelunk-challenge chests, so its value is camp-dependent (Missions footnote 2).

camps.name: Canyon of the Lost Excursion
camps.notes: By far the easiest camp — short caves, weak enemies.

camps.name: Molten Heights Hike
camps.notes: Smallest playable area, easily accessible challenges and many easily reachable environmental chests. Second-best chrono camp.

camps.name: Sky Islands Exploration
camps.notes: Requires aerial movement. Falling no longer ends runs.

camps.name: The Corkus Traversal
camps.notes: Biggest playable area with long convoluted caves. BEST chrono camp — many multi-chest Spelunk challenges plus a high density of environmental chests.

camps.name: Silent Expanse Expedition
camps.notes: First major difficulty jump; scaling comparable to Fruma.

camps.name: The Fruma Foray — West
camps.notes: Big enemy swarms and tight caves. Described as the most annoying. Recommended for Yellow (flying chest) runs.

camps.name: The Fruma Foray — East
camps.notes: Currently the toughest camp by far. Recommended for Yellow runs.

spelunkChallenges.note: Named multi-step Spelunk challenges. Their chests DO count toward Chronokinesis.

gap: The guide gives difficulty ordering and qualitative chest descriptions but NOT quantified environmental-chest density or challenge-type mix. Chronokinesis scoring is therefore ordinal (Corkus > Molten Heights > rest), not numeric.
